item.description:
PDFBrochure 0.6

Polish update — the app looks better, behaves more deliberately, and is now backed by an automated test suite.

  • Green book icon. Replaces the previous black-on-white glyph.
  • Result screen after creating. Clicking Create Brochure now switches to a dedicated screen that shows a spinner while the PDF is generated, then offers Reveal in Finder or Make another brochure. On error, the screen offers Try again (keeping your loaded PDF and settings) or Start over.
  • Cleaner drop screen. The redundant title is gone; the call-to-action "Drop a PDF to make a brochure" now lives directly inside the drop zone.
  • Smarter paper-size detection. US Letter input no longer gets mistakenly treated as A4. The previous heuristic compared total area, which placed Letter and A4 within 3% of each other; the new check compares both width and height, so each format routes to its native sheet size.

Behind the scenes: the SwiftUI source was split into one-file-per-screen with #Preview blocks, and a 35-test suite now covers both the saddle-stitch imposition algorithm (verified by rendering output sheets and pixel-sampling each slot) and the new drop / layout / result state machine.

Apple Silicon only. Requires macOS 13 or later.

PDFBrochure 0.5

First public release. PDFBrochure turns any PDF into a saddle-stitch booklet ready to print double-sided and fold — two source pages laid out per sheet, in the right order.

  • Drop a PDF, get a brochure. Drag-and-drop a PDF onto the window (or click to pick one). Pick a sheet size and page placement, see a live preview update as you change them, then click Create Brochure to write <input>-brochure.pdf next to the source. The app never overwrites an existing file — it auto-suffixes -2, -3, … on collision.
  • Smart paper-size default. A4 input becomes A4 landscape (two A5 pages per sheet); A5 input becomes A4 landscape at native size; other sizes default to auto (input width doubled).
  • Imposition rules in plain English. For an N-page document padded up to a multiple of 4, sheet k holds (N−2k+2 | 2k−1) on the outer side and (2k | N−2k+1) on the inner side. Print double-sided (flip on short edge), nest the sheets, fold along the spine, staple. Pages beyond the original count are left blank.
  • Headless CLI included. A small pdfbrochurecli tool ships in the repo's build for scripting and tests.
  • Auto-update support. Future releases land via Sparkle's "Check for Updates…" menu item, with EdDSA-signed update artefacts.

Apple Silicon only. Requires macOS 13 or later.
